hook_question: SHE FILED OFF HER OWN\NFINGERPRINTS. THEN\NSOMEONE BURNED HER.
iconic_detail: An unidentified woman found burned in a remote Norwegian valley, carrying multiple false passports and a coded notebook; suspected Cold War espionage, never identified

chapters.voiceover:
November 1970. Isdalen valley, Norway — locals call it Death Valley.

Hikers find a woman's body, burned almost beyond recognition.

Her fingertips have been filed down, one by one. Every clothing label cut out by hand.

In her hotel rooms, police find sleeping pills with the serial numbers scratched off, and passports — five of them, five different names, each forged well enough to cross a border.

Then they find her notebook: pages of coded numbers, tracking a route through a dozen European cities.

Investigators quietly suspect a Cold War operative. Fifty-six years later, the Isdal Woman still has no name.
